verdict.label: Marginal
verdict.headline: Supertubos Lunch
verdict.reason: Supertubos is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide is lining up for a powerful wedge, so keep the comfort level in mind.

best.bestWind: ideal N/NNE/NE, usable NNW/ENE, clean <=22 km/h

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Supertubos looks like a clean small window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 77 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 58/100, and good wind at 20 km/h.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort; 10-13s at mid tide is the prime barrel / wedge setup for Supertubos / Molhe Leste.  Watch: spot surf looks too small.
best.shortExplanation: Supertubos is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide is lining up for a powerful wedge, so keep the comfort level in mind.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a fun-sized window for Fun progression, Low confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 99 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 62/100, and wind is the main risk.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small; 10-13s at mid tide suits the reef shelves and gives long workable walls.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Lagide is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should help the reef hold shape.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

photo.alt: Photo of Molhe Leste surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Molhe Leste looks like a clean small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 77 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 58/100, and good wind at 20 km/h.  A jetty-shaped beach break with punchy rights and lefts; this is best for sheltered power when Supertubos has too much size; 10-13s at mid tide is the prime barrel / wedge setup for Supertubos / Molhe Leste.  Watch: spot surf looks too small; Molhe Leste looks below your bodyboard fun size.
ranked.shortExplanation: Molhe Leste is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide is lining up for a powerful wedge, so keep the comfort level in mind.
warnings: Molhe Leste looks below your bodyboard fun size

photo.alt: Photo of Meio da Baia surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Meio da Baia looks like a fun-sized window for Worth checking, Low confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 108 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 62/100, and wind is the main risk.  An inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay; this is best for bodyboard sessions when the bay is clean, there is enough period and size, and the forecast shows a defined wave; 10-13s at mid tide works well on exposed beaches.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Meio da Baia is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
